description: 60F, LDL 195 (≥190), CKD G4 (eGFR 22), ACR 145. ACC/AHA severe hypercholesterolemia R2 says high-intensity statin. KDIGO statin-for-CKD R3 MODIFIES intensity to moderate (M2) due to CKD G4 pharmacokinetics and myopathy risk. This exercises the second MODIFIES edge that case-03 doesn't reach (M2 targets severe hypercholesterolemia, not secondary prevention).

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA R2 (COR I, LOE B-NR): LDL ≥190, age 20-75, no ASCVD — high-intensity statin indicated. However, KDIGO R3 (1A) MODIFIES intensity to moderate for CKD G4 (eGFR 22) due to altered pharmacokinetics and increased myopathy risk at high-intensity doses (M2 edge, ADR 0018). Consider atorvastatin 10-20mg or rosuvastatin 5-10mg — lower doses than standard high-intensity to account for reduced renal clearance.

actions.label: Monitor eGFR and urine ACR per KDIGO
actions.rationale: KDIGO R1 (1B): regular monitoring of eGFR and urine ACR. CKD G4 (eGFR 22) with A2 albuminuria (ACR 145) indicates very high risk. Monitor every 3-6 months at this stage.

actions.label: Continue ARB therapy for albuminuric CKD
actions.rationale: Patient already on losartan (ARB) for CKD with albuminuria (ACR 145, A2). Satisfies KDIGO R4 (1B). Continue and titrate to maximally tolerated dose.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure to <130/80
actions.rationale: BP 142/88 above target. CKD G4 + albuminuria requires aggressive BP control. Target <130/80 per KDIGO. Losartan dose may need uptitration; consider adding a second agent.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend high-intensity statin despite LDL ≥190
contraindications.rationale: KDIGO R3 (1A) modifies ACC/AHA R2's high-intensity recommendation to moderate-intensity in CKD G4 (eGFR 22). High-intensity statins have altered pharmacokinetics in advanced CKD with reduced clearance leading to higher plasma levels and increased myopathy risk (M2 edge). The KDIGO modifier takes precedence for dosing intensity.

contraindications.label: Do not initiate SGLT2 inhibitor
contraindications.rationale: eGFR 22 is ≥20 (KDIGO threshold for initiation), but ACR 145 is below the 200 mg/g threshold for non-diabetic SGLT2 use, and patient has no diabetes. KDIGO SGLT2 R2 requires either (CKD + diabetes) or (ACR ≥200). Neither pathway is met.
